prompt: 2026 CURRENT pricing + offering for AI-SPECIFIC security / red-teaming / governance products: LAKERA, PROTECT AI, HIDDENLAYER, MINDGARD, CREDO AI, CRANIUM, and any 'AI agent security' platforms (Zenity, Operant, Obsidian). For each (cite URLs/dates): (a) pricing if public or model (usually enterprise/annual), (b) what you GET (runtime guardrails/firewall, model scanning, red-teaming, AI governance/risk register, agent security posture), (c) is it a runtime DEFENSE product or does it produce EVIDENCE/an audit a buyer can use to clear a security review, (d) does it produce a SIGNED, offline-VERIFIABLE report, (e) is it on-demand/self-serve or enterprise-sales. Distinguish 'defends the AI at runtime' vs 'produces buyer-verifiable evidence for the sale'.
content:
**Most of these vendors operate on custom/enterprise pricing models (typically annual contracts, often six figures+ depending on scale, models/agents protected, and features), with limited or no public self-serve tiers beyond trials or limited free/community access. Pricing details are rarely transparent and usually require sales contact or demos. Information is current as of mid-2026 based on official sites, AWS Marketplace listings, reviews, and analyses (e.g., eesel.ai breakdowns).**[[1]](https://www.eesel.ai/blog/lakera-pricing)[[2]](https://www.eesel.ai/blog/protect-ai-pricing)

**Key distinctions**:
- **Runtime defense**: Real-time guardrails/firewalls, prompt injection/jailbreak blocking, data leakage prevention, behavioral controls during inference/execution (e.g., Lakera Guard, Protect AI Layer, Mindgard runtime protection, Zenity Defend, Operant AI Gatekeeper).
- **Evidence/audit for security reviews**: Model scanning/BOMs, red-teaming reports, risk registers, compliance mappings (e.g., EU AI Act, NIST), posture assessments, or audit-ready outputs. Some produce verifiable reports; few emphasize cryptographically signed/offline-verifiable ones (more common in governance tools).
- **Sales model**: Predominantly enterprise-sales (demos, custom quotes, annual contracts). Self-serve is rare/limited.

### Lakera (lakera.ai)
- **(a) Pricing**: Community plan free (up to 10k API requests/month, basic features). Enterprise/Pro custom (contact sales; usage-based elements possible). No public tiered pricing beyond free tier.[[1]](https://www.eesel.ai/blog/lakera-pricing)[[3]](https://platform.lakera.ai/pricing)
- **(b) Offerings**: Runtime guardrails/firewall (Lakera Guard for prompt attacks, data leakage, jailbreaks; multimodal/model-agnostic; low-latency); AI agent security (runtime protection); red-teaming insights via Gandalf platform (community-driven); workforce/shadow AI discovery and policy controls. Strong on real-time threat detection and adaptive protection.[[4]](https://www.lakera.ai/)
- **(c)**: Primarily runtime **DEFENSE** product (real-time protection integrated via API). Also supports evidence via threat data and policies.
- **(d)**: No strong emphasis on signed/offline-verifiable reports (focus is operational runtime).
- **(e)**: Hybrid—self-serve Community tier available; full features via enterprise sales/demo.[[5]](https://www.lakera.ai/careers)

### Protect AI (protectai.com; acquired by Palo Alto Networks ~2025)
- **(a) Pricing**: Custom enterprise (no public tiers). AWS Marketplace lists placeholder $1/unit for modules (Recon red-teaming, Radar AI BOM, Guardian model scanning, Layer runtime), but real pricing is negotiated/contract-based.[[6]](https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-lkyeukow5zsfc)[[2]](https://www.eesel.ai/blog/protect-ai-pricing)
- **(b)**: End-to-end—Guardian (model scanning/security), Recon (scalable GenAI red-teaming), Layer (runtime LLM monitoring/management/guardrails), Radar (AI BOM). Covers model scanning, red-teaming, runtime defense, and agent security. Unified platform with threat research backing.[[7]](https://protectai.com/)
- **(c)**: Both runtime **DEFENSE** (Layer) and **EVIDENCE** (scanning, red-teaming reports, BOMs for audits/reviews).
- **(d)**: Produces reports/artifacts from scanning/red-teaming (not explicitly signed/offline-verifiable highlighted).
- **(e)**: Enterprise-sales only (demos requested; targets production AI deployments).[[8]](https://workos.com/blog/protect-ai-vs-workos-runtime-detection-enterprise-authentication)

### HiddenLayer (hiddenlayer.com)
- **(a) Pricing**: Custom enterprise. AWS Marketplace example: ~$5M for full 12-month AISec Platform access (high-end; likely for large deployments). Modular capabilities priced via contract.[[9]](https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-2haypjrayfxgw)
- **(b)**: End-to-end AI security platform—model scanner, AI detection/response, automated red-teaming, security posture management. Covers discovery, supply chain, attack simulation, runtime security. Agentic/generative/predictive AI support across MLOps lifecycle.[[10]](https://marketplace.microsoft.com/en-us/product/saas/hiddenlayerinc1690422428200.hiddenlayer-model-scanner?tab=overview)
- **(c)**: Both runtime **DEFENSE** and **EVIDENCE** (scanning, posture, red-teaming outputs for reviews).
- **(d)**: Platform supports reporting for compliance/audits (specific signed/offline details not emphasized).
- **(e)**: Enterprise-sales (demos, marketplace contracts).

### Mindgard (mindgard.ai)
- **(a) Pricing**: Free sandbox/test drive (AI Security Labs). Enterprise custom (contact for production).[[11]](https://sourceforge.net/software/product/Mindgard/)
- **(b)**: Automated AI red-teaming & security testing platform. Agent-native reconnaissance, exploit discovery, risk assessment, runtime protection/hardening, guardrails. Covers models, agents, applications; attacker-aligned scanning and compliance reporting. Strong on agent security posture and vulnerabilities.[[12]](https://mindgard.ai/)
- **(c)**: Both—red-teaming/**EVIDENCE** (reports, risk assessments) and runtime **DEFENSE**/response.
- **(d)**: Produces AI security risk/compliance reports (actionable outputs; signed/offline not specifically highlighted).
- **(e)**: Self-serve sandbox + enterprise sales for full platform.

### Credo AI (credo.ai)
- **(a) Pricing**: Enterprise custom (typically $100K+/year starting; subscription based on AI use cases/systems). AWS Marketplace private offers.[[13]](https://verifywise.ai/blog/credo-ai-vs-verifywise-2025-comparison-which-ai-governance-platform-is-right-for-you)[[14]](https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-x67krdatcdday)
- **(b)**: AI governance/risk register platform—discover AI, policy enforcement, compliance proof (EU AI Act, NIST, etc.), risk management, runtime monitoring, audit-ready reporting. Focus on governance, impact assessments, and continuous oversight for models/agents.[[15]](https://www.credo.ai/product)
- **(c)**: Primarily **EVIDENCE**/audit for security reviews and compliance (governance workflows, dashboards, reports). Includes runtime monitoring elements.
- **(d)**: Strong on audit-ready documentation and reporting.
- **(e)**: Enterprise-sales only (demos, personalized).

### Cranium (cranium.ai)
- **(a) Pricing**: Tiered/custom enterprise subscription (annual; details via sales or integrations like Archer). ROI studies reference enterprise deployments.[[16]](https://www.gartner.com/reviews/product/cranium-1160091663)
- **(b)**: AI security, governance, and agentic AI platform—visibility into systems, risk identification, compliance demonstration (internal/3rd-party), security posture. Maps threats/vulnerabilities; supports agentic capabilities.[[17]](https://cranium.ai/)
- **(c)**: Both runtime/security controls and **EVIDENCE** (compliance, risk registers, posture for reviews).
- **(d)**: Supports compliance reporting and demonstrations.
- **(e)**: Enterprise-sales (demos emphasized).

### AI Agent Security Platforms
- **Zenity (zenity.io)**: Custom enterprise pricing (no public tiers; demo/quote required). Focus: AI agent security & governance—Observe (observability), Govern (posture management), Defend (runtime policies/threat response). Full lifecycle (buildtime to runtime) for agents across platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, Salesforce). Strong on agent-specific posture, data loss prevention, and adaptive security. Primarily runtime **DEFENSE** + governance **EVIDENCE**. Enterprise-sales.[[18]](https://zenity.io/)[[19]](https://workos.com/blog/zenity-vs-workos-agentic-security)
- **Operant (operant.ai)**: Usage-based pricing (predictable, scales with stack/usage; self-serve start options). Real-time AI security (AI Gatekeeper/runtime defense against prompt attacks, etc.), API threat protection, red-teaming (Woodpecker). Agentic AI ecosystem focus with visibility, enforcement, guardrails. Strong runtime **DEFENSE**; some evidence via protection logs. Mix of self-serve/usage + enterprise.[[20]](https://www.operant.ai/platform/pricing)
- **Obsidian (obsidiansecurity.com)**: Custom/enterprise (SSPM platform extension). AI agent security via visibility, access governance, privilege management, behavioral monitoring, and runtime controls in SaaS environments. Focus on discovery, posture (excessive access cleanup), and stopping high-risk actions. Primarily **EVIDENCE**/governance + runtime elements. Enterprise-sales.[[21]](https://www.obsidiansecurity.com/ai-agent-security)

**Summary notes**: Runtime-focused tools (Lakera, Protect Layer, Zenity Defend, Operant, Mindgard runtime) defend in production. Governance/evidence tools (Credo, Cranium, scanning/red-teaming modules) help clear reviews with artifacts/reports. Few publicly detail cryptographically signed/offline-verifiable reports (more common in formal compliance platforms). All lean enterprise-sales except limited free tiers or usage-based options. Verify latest via vendor demos, as offerings evolve rapidly.
